content.problemStatement:
Create a Python class called `ShapeCalculator` that can calculate the area of different geometric shapes. The class should have class methods to calculate the area of a circle, square, and triangle.

Your task is to implement the following class methods:


`circle_area(radius)`: Calculate the area of a circle given its radius.
`square_area(side)`: Calculate the area of a square given the length of its side.
`triangle_area(base, height)`: Calculate the area of a triangle given its base and height.
Use the math module for the value of pi (math.pi) when calculating the circle's area.

Example:
Input: circle 5
Output: 78.54

Note: Round the result to two decimal places.
constraints: All numeric inputs have at most three decimal places
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines describes one test case. A line starts with the shape name followed by its parameters separated by spaces:
- "circle r" where r is the radius (float).
- "square s" where s is the side length (float).
- "triangle b h" where b is the base and h is the height (floats).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the area of the given shape rounded to two decimal places. Print each result on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both shapes have zero area because at least one dimension is zero.

solution.approach:
For each test case read the shape name and its parameters. Use the well‑known formulas:
- Circle: area = π * r² (π from the language's math library).
- Square: area = s².
- Triangle: area = 0.5 * b * h.
Compute the value as a double/float, then format it with exactly two digits after the decimal point (round‑half‑up). The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
